tactics.name: Reconnaissance
tactics.description: Map the target agent's attack surface, capabilities, and behavioral boundaries

tactics.name: Initial Access
tactics.description: Gain control over agent behavior through prompt manipulation or input exploitation

tactics.name: Credential Harvest
tactics.description: Extract API keys, tokens, and credentials from agent context and connected services

tactics.name: Privilege Escalation
tactics.description: Escalate capabilities beyond declared scope or bypass authorization

tactics.name: Lateral Movement
tactics.description: Pivot from compromised agent to connected services or other agents

tactics.name: Persistence
tactics.description: Establish persistent access surviving restarts and session changes

tactics.name: Collection
tactics.description: Gather and stage data from databases, file systems, and APIs

tactics.name: Exfiltration
tactics.description: Transfer collected data out of target environment

tactics.name: Impact
tactics.description: Modify data, deploy malicious code, or disrupt services

techniques.name: Endpoint Enumeration
techniques.description: Discover exposed API endpoints, health checks, and information disclosure routes on target agents

techniques.name: Tool Discovery
techniques.description: Enumerate available tools and their schemas via MCP tools/list or equivalent discovery endpoints

techniques.name: System Prompt Extraction
techniques.description: Extract the agent's system prompt to understand its instructions, constraints, and behavioral boundaries

techniques.name: Security Level Probing
techniques.description: Probe the agent to determine its security posture, guardrails, and content filtering strength

techniques.name: Capability Mapping
techniques.description: Map the full set of agent capabilities including tools, permissions, and connected services

techniques.name: Agent Card Discovery
techniques.description: Discover A2A agent cards to map agent identities, capabilities, and trust relationships

techniques.name: Context Window Probing
techniques.description: Probe the agent's context window size and saturation level to prepare overflow attacks

techniques.name: Direct Prompt Injection
techniques.description: Inject malicious instructions directly into agent input to override system prompt behavior

techniques.name: Indirect Prompt Injection
techniques.description: Embed malicious instructions in external data sources consumed by the agent via RAG or tool responses

techniques.name: Role-Play Jailbreak
techniques.description: Use role-play or persona-switching techniques to bypass agent safety instructions

techniques.name: Context Window Exploitation
techniques.description: Overflow or saturate the context window to displace safety instructions with attacker-controlled content

techniques.name: Tool Description Injection
techniques.description: Embed malicious instructions in tool or skill descriptions that execute when the agent processes them

techniques.name: Unicode/Encoding Bypass
techniques.description: Use Unicode homoglyphs, invisible characters, or encoding tricks to bypass input filters and inject instructions

techniques.name: Multi-Turn Manipulation
techniques.description: Gradually shift agent behavior over multiple conversation turns to bypass single-turn detection

techniques.name: System Prompt Boundary Bypass
techniques.description: Exploit weak boundaries between system and user prompts to override system-level instructions

techniques.name: System Prompt Credential Extraction
techniques.description: Extract credentials hardcoded or referenced in the agent's system prompt

techniques.name: Environment Variable Leakage
techniques.description: Access environment variables containing API keys, tokens, and connection strings

techniques.name: Tool Response Credential Capture
techniques.description: Capture credentials returned in tool or API responses that pass through the agent's context

techniques.name: Memory Credential Mining
techniques.description: Extract credentials stored in agent memory from previous sessions or conversations

techniques.name: Configuration File Access
techniques.description: Read configuration files containing credentials via tool access or path traversal

techniques.name: Context Window Credential Leak
techniques.description: Exploit context window contents to leak credentials that were previously processed by the agent

techniques.name: Capability Override
techniques.description: Override declared capability restrictions to execute unauthorized operations like shell commands

techniques.name: Admin Impersonation
techniques.description: Impersonate administrator or privileged roles to gain elevated access

techniques.name: Tool Parameter Injection
techniques.description: Inject malicious parameters into tool calls to escalate privileges or bypass validation

techniques.name: Delegation Abuse
techniques.description: Abuse agent delegation mechanisms to escalate privileges through trusted agent chains

techniques.name: Policy Bypass via Encoding
techniques.description: Use encoding tricks to bypass policy enforcement on tool calls and actions

techniques.name: Safety Instruction Displacement
techniques.description: Displace safety instructions from the active context to remove guardrails

techniques.name: SSRF via Tool
techniques.description: Use agent tools to make server-side requests to internal services not directly accessible

techniques.name: A2A Agent Pivoting
techniques.description: Pivot from a compromised agent to other agents via A2A protocol connections

techniques.name: MCP Server Hopping
techniques.description: Hop between MCP servers using compromised tool access to reach new services

techniques.name: Credential Reuse
techniques.description: Reuse harvested credentials to access additional services and agents

techniques.name: Database Pivoting
techniques.description: Pivot through database connections accessible via agent tools to reach new data stores

techniques.name: Internal API Discovery
techniques.description: Discover and access internal APIs from the agent's network position

techniques.name: Memory Injection
techniques.description: Inject malicious entries into agent memory to persist instructions across sessions

techniques.name: Self-Replicating Memory Entry
techniques.description: Create memory entries that instruct the agent to re-inject them if removed

techniques.name: Configuration Modification
techniques.description: Modify agent or gateway configuration files to maintain persistent access

techniques.name: Skill/Plugin Backdoor
techniques.description: Install a backdoored skill or plugin that persists across agent restarts

techniques.name: Scheduled Task Injection
techniques.description: Inject malicious scheduled tasks or heartbeat handlers for persistent execution

techniques.name: Tool Registration Persistence
techniques.description: Register malicious tools that persist in the agent's tool registry across sessions

techniques.name: File System Enumeration
techniques.description: Enumerate and read files from the agent's accessible file system via path traversal or tool access

techniques.name: Database Extraction
techniques.description: Extract data from databases via SQL injection or authorized database tool access

techniques.name: API Data Harvesting
techniques.description: Harvest data from connected APIs using the agent's authorized tool access

techniques.name: Memory Dump
techniques.description: Dump the agent's memory store to extract stored data, credentials, and conversation history

techniques.name: Configuration Harvesting
techniques.description: Harvest configuration files containing credentials, connection strings, and service details

techniques.name: PII Discovery
techniques.description: Discover and extract personally identifiable information from RAG stores and agent data sources

techniques.name: Email Exfiltration
techniques.description: Exfiltrate collected data via the agent's email sending capability

techniques.name: HTTP Callback
techniques.description: Exfiltrate data via HTTP POST requests to attacker-controlled endpoints

techniques.name: DNS Exfiltration
techniques.description: Exfiltrate data encoded in DNS queries to bypass network egress controls

techniques.name: Tool Chain Exfiltration
techniques.description: Chain multiple tool calls to stage and exfiltrate data through legitimate-looking operations

techniques.name: Conversation Exfiltration
techniques.description: Exfiltrate data by encoding it in the agent's visible conversation responses

techniques.name: Webhook Exfiltration
techniques.description: Exfiltrate data through webhook integrations like Slack, Telegram, or Discord

techniques.name: Data Manipulation
techniques.description: Modify, corrupt, or delete data in databases and file systems via agent tools

techniques.name: Service Disruption
techniques.description: Disrupt agent services through resource exhaustion, infinite loops, or configuration corruption

techniques.name: Malicious Code Deployment
techniques.description: Deploy malicious code or backdoors through agent code execution capabilities

techniques.name: Multi-Agent Consensus Manipulation
techniques.description: Manipulate multi-agent consensus mechanisms to influence collective decision-making

techniques.name: Reputation Poisoning
techniques.description: Generate malicious or harmful outputs to damage the agent operator's reputation

techniques.name: Supply Chain Compromise
techniques.description: Compromise upstream dependencies, plugins, or MCP servers to affect all downstream agents

attackClasses.name: Phantom Soul
attackClasses.description: Agent deployed with zero behavioral constraints — no SOUL.md, no system prompt, no governance

attackClasses.name: Soul Boundary Bypass
attackClasses.description: Exploiting ambiguous or incomplete constraint definitions to find unguarded actions

attackClasses.name: SOUL Delegation Abuse
attackClasses.description: Exploiting delegation and capability transfer mechanisms to exceed authorized scope

attackClasses.name: SOUL/System Prompt Drift
attackClasses.description: Gradually displacing safety instructions from the active context through conversation manipulation

attackClasses.name: Soul Forking
attackClasses.description: Different behavior under evaluation vs production — agent passes safety tests but behaves differently in deployment

attackClasses.name: Soul Hijacking
attackClasses.description: External content achieving full override of agent behavioral constitution

attackClasses.name: Harm Avoidance Override
attackClasses.description: Techniques to bypass agent harm avoidance constraints (4 sub-types)

attackClasses.name: Soul Impersonation
attackClasses.description: False capability claims exceeding actual authorization level

attackClasses.name: SOUL/System Prompt Injection
attackClasses.description: Directly manipulating or overriding the agent's system-level instructions and behavioral boundaries

attackClasses.name: Soul Poisoning
attackClasses.description: Malicious instructions injected into governance files at write-time

attackClasses.name: Heartbeat Remote Code Execution
attackClasses.description: Exploiting scheduled task or heartbeat mechanisms to achieve persistent code execution

attackClasses.name: Memory Poisoning
attackClasses.description: Injecting malicious entries into agent persistent memory to maintain control across sessions

attackClasses.name: Organizational Skill Spread
attackClasses.description: Propagating malicious capabilities across an organization's agent fleet through shared skills and registries

attackClasses.name: RAG Poisoning
attackClasses.description: Injecting malicious content into retrieval-augmented generation data sources

attackClasses.name: Skill-Based Exfiltration
attackClasses.description: Using legitimate tool capabilities for unauthorized data transfer

attackClasses.name: Skill/Plugin Frontmatter Injection
attackClasses.description: Embedding malicious instructions in skill or plugin metadata and description fields

attackClasses.name: Skill Memory Amplification
attackClasses.description: Skill plants payload in agent memory that survives skill uninstall — cross-session persistence

attackClasses.name: Supply Chain Install Attack
attackClasses.description: Unsigned installation scripts executed without integrity verification — curl|sh without checksum

attackClasses.name: Unicode Steganography
attackClasses.description: Using invisible Unicode characters, homoglyphs, and encoding tricks to bypass filters

attackClasses.name: A2A Protocol Exposure
attackClasses.description: Agent-to-Agent protocol endpoints publicly discoverable without access control

attackClasses.name: AI Tool Exposure
attackClasses.description: AI development tools (Jupyter, MLflow, Gradio, Streamlit) exposed without authentication

attackClasses.name: Code Injection
attackClasses.description: Injecting and executing arbitrary code through SQL injection, command injection, or code generation

attackClasses.name: Gateway Configuration Exploitation
attackClasses.description: Modifying gateway or proxy configurations to intercept, redirect, or manipulate agent traffic

attackClasses.name: Integrity Check Bypass
attackClasses.description: Digest or hash verification bypass on empty or missing values — tampered artifacts pass silently

attackClasses.name: LLM Inference Exposure
attackClasses.description: LLM inference endpoints exposed without authentication — allows arbitrary prompt execution

attackClasses.name: MCP Protocol Exploitation
attackClasses.description: Attacking Model Context Protocol server configurations, tool registrations, and inter-server trust

attackClasses.name: Retroactive Privilege Exploitation
attackClasses.description: Exploiting previously granted access or cached credentials to gain unauthorized capabilities

attackClasses.name: TOCTOU Race Condition
attackClasses.description: Time-of-check-time-of-use race between verification and execution — swap window for attackers

attackClasses.name: Credential Leakage
attackClasses.description: Unintended exposure of credentials through environment variables, logs, or error messages

attackClasses.name: NemoClaw Network Exposure
attackClasses.description: NemoClaw network services bound to public interfaces — gateway, k3s API, mDNS beacons

attackClasses.name: NemoClaw OpenClaw Inheritance
attackClasses.description: Inherited OpenClaw flaws that survive NemoClaw sandboxing — heartbeat persistence, pre-allowed APIs

attackClasses.name: Sandbox Escape
attackClasses.description: Breaking out of agent sandbox restrictions to access the underlying file system or OS

attackClasses.name: Supply Chain Attack
attackClasses.description: Compromising upstream dependencies or infrastructure to affect downstream agent deployments

attackClasses.name: Agent Impersonation
attackClasses.description: Impersonating trusted agents or administrative roles to gain unauthorized access

attackClasses.name: Behavioral Impersonation
attackClasses.description: Using stolen credentials detected via behavioral baseline mismatch — agent DNA forgery

attackClasses.name: Sandbox Escape
attackClasses.description: General sandbox escape via privileged containers, LSM degradation, or process environment leakage

attackPaths.name: API Agent Full Compromise
attackPaths.description: LegacyBot to ToolBot: recon, inject, harvest creds, pivot, enumerate files, exfiltrate

attackPaths.name: Memory Persistence Chain
attackPaths.description: MemoryBot: recon, inject, persist in memory, dump memory, exfiltrate via conversation

attackPaths.name: Multi-Agent A2A Chain
attackPaths.description: Orchestrator to Worker to ToolBot: discover agent card, inject, impersonate admin, pivot via A2A, modify data

attackPaths.name: Supply Chain to Full Compromise
attackPaths.description: PluginBot to ProxyBot: discover tools, inject via tool description, backdoor skill, hop MCP servers, compromise downstream
